articles.title: Introducing LibPDF: PDF Parsing and Generation from TypeScript
articles.description: LibPDF bills itself as ‘the PDF library TypeScript deserves’ and supports parsing, modifying, signing and generating PDFs with a modern API in Node, Bun, and the browser. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Documenso

articles.title: JavaScript Frameworks – Heading into 2026
articles.description: The creator of SolidJS knows more than a thing or two about JS frameworks and has written an annual review of the scene for the past few years. Here, he picks on four areas of evolution, and says it’s “an incredibly exciting time to be working on JavaScript frameworks.”
articles.author: Ryan Carniato

articles.title: Porting 100k Lines from TypeScript to Rust in a Month
articles.description: A prolific JavaScript developer ported a Pokémon battle simulator to Rust and shares his experiences and techniques used to work around issues where Claude Code would get bogged down in such a large task. He notes “LLM-based coding agents are such a great new tool” but require “engineering expertise and constant babysitting”.
articles.author: Christopher Chedeau

articles.title: Building a JavaScript Runtime in One Month
articles.description: “What if I could build a JavaScript engine small enough to embed in a C program, but complete enough to actually run real code?” The end result is Ant.
articles.author: theMackabu

articles.title: Inside Turbopack: Building Faster by Building Less
articles.description: If you’re working on a large codebase, faster hot reloading, better scaling, and persistent caching are all quite desirable. Here’s how these things came about in Turbopack.
articles.author: Shew, Woodruff and Koppers (Vercel)

articles.title: ▶  Bun Explained in 100 Seconds
articles.description: The popular quick dev explainer channel tackles Bun at a high level.
articles.author: Fireship

articles.title: Midscene.js: Remote Control for the Web, Mobile and Desktop Using Vision Models
articles.description: Provides a way to drive numerous platforms from JavaScript (including iOS) by using various integrations and a vision-capable model so you can write actions in a mixture of JavaScript and natural language and have them performed.
articles.author: ByteDance Inc.

articles.title: 🔄 Travels 1.0: A Fast, Framework-Agnostic Undo/Redo Library
articles.description: Allows you to add undo/redo functionality to apps like text editors, drawing tools, or other interactive software. Uses a memory efficient technique only storing changes, rather than full snapshots for each change.
articles.author: Mutative

articles.title: SonicJS 2.7: Perf-Focused Edge-Native Headless CMS for Cloudflare Workers
articles.description: A production-ready CMS built specifically for the edge. GitHub repo.
articles.author: SonicJS Team

articles.title: 🤖 Mastra 1.0: An AI Framework from the Former Gatsby Team
articles.description: An all-in-one framework (homepage) for building AI-powered apps and agents.
articles.author: Sam Bhagwat

articles.description: Lea Verou has celebrated getting two ECMAScript proposals she's championed to stage 1 at TC39 this week: Composable Accessors and Alias Accessors. Rob Palmer also shares more proposal updates from the latest TC39 plenary.
🕹️ The creator of Three.js (known as mrdoob) has created a Three.js-powered port of 1996's Quake – here's the source. You can learn more about this project in this X thread.
📊 JSBenchmarks.com is a fresh attempt to benchmark several popular JavaScript frameworks. As always with benchmarks, use a critical eye, but the example apps are open to read or contribute to.
Node.js 25.5.0 (Current) – Introduces a --build-sea option that simplifies the process of building single executable applications.

Bun v1.3.7 – The popular runtime updates its JavaScriptCore engine, leading to 35% faster async/await and ARM64 perf improvements. It also lands a new option to generate profiling data in Markdown format for easier sharing, plus native JSON5 and JSONL parsing support.
Rolldown 1.0 RC – Fast Rust-based bundler with a Rollup-compatible API and esbuild feature parity.
npm v11.8.0, Emscripten 5.0, Neutralinojs 6.5.0

articles.title: When Will CSS Grid Lanes Arrive? How Long Until We Can Use It?
articles.description: As we shared earlier this month, CSS Grid Lanes is arriving soon across browsers (with Safari already shipping support in Technology Preview). Here’s a look at the current implementation status and three approaches for progressively enhancing your layouts to use Grid Lanes today while still supporting older browsers.
articles.author: Jen Simmons (WebKit)

articles.title: Understanding the Fundamentals of CSS Layout
articles.description: A solid guide to demystifying some foundational CSS concepts (flow, box model, positioning, etc), all in an effort to help aid our overall understanding of the language. There’s a video version too if you’d prefer.
articles.author: Kilian Valkhof

articles.title: Try Text Scaling Support in Chrome Canary
articles.description: The new text-scale meta tag is now available for testing behind a flag in Canary. It’s designed to respect a user’s text size preferences on the web, and essentially handle scaling in a way that simply zooming often fails at. Josh sums it up: “the <meta name="text-scale"> tells the browser, ‘Hey, I’ve designed my website to still work if the user increases their OS text size’”.
articles.author: Josh Tumath

articles.title: CSS in 2026: New Features Reshaping Frontend Development
articles.description: A laundry list of some of the latest features either now available or on the way, along with how we can go about implementing them in our codebase.
articles.author: Jemima Abu

articles.title: How to Favicon in 2026: Three Files That Fit Most Needs
articles.description: Asks us to “stop the icon generator madness” and take a smarter approach, adopting a minimal set of icons that fits most modern needs.
articles.author: Andrey Sitnik

articles.title: 🦋 'I Added a Bluesky Comment Section to My Blog'
articles.description: Details the process of fetching and displaying replies from the social network into a statically generated blog via the Bluesky API.
articles.author: Micah Cantor

articles.title: More Invoker Commands and More Reasons Not to Use JavaScript Please
articles.description: A look at the baseline-supported Invoker Commands API, which enables declarative HTML controls for dialogs and popovers without the need for JavaScript event listeners.
articles.author: Paweł Grzybek

articles.title: Beyond the Mouse: Animating with Mobile Accelerometers
articles.description: Open this one on your phone to best enjoy the various code examples and demos.
articles.author: Amit Sheen

articles.title: There's No Need to Trap Focus on a Dialog Element
articles.description: A quick FYI on tabbing out of a <dialog>, and ‘deprecated’ advice on focus-trapping.
articles.author: Zell Liew

articles.title: Unstacking CSS Stacking Contexts
articles.description: A solid look at how elements are stacked on a page, what controls the stacking order, and practical approaches to “unstack” elements when needed.
articles.author: Gabriel Shoyombo

articles.title: 🎨 Color Palette Pro: Color Palette Tool with a 'Synth-Like' UI
articles.description: Its skeuomorphic design mirrors that of a would-be hardware device. Supports numerous color spaces, six palette types, and results are shareable. Use the live color picker to grab any color for a palette starting point. Fun to play with!
articles.author: Color Palette Pro

articles.title: Introducing ReliCSS: A Tool for Frontend Archaeology
articles.description: Stuart recently launched ReliCSS, an online tool that carries out some 'CSS archaeology', and finds those browser hacks you no longer need.
articles.author: Stuart Robson

articles.title: 📄 LibPDF: PDF Parsing and Generation from TypeScript
articles.description: LibPDF bills itself as ‘the PDF library TypeScript deserves’ and supports parsing, modifying, signing and generating PDFs with a modern API in Node, Bun, and the browser. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Documenso

articles.title: SolidUI: An Unofficial Port of shadcn/ui and Tremor to SolidJS
articles.description: Most of the usual elements are here (dashboard, cards, auth, etc.), ported from two popular component libraries to Solid. They're ready to copy and paste with no need to install as a dependency. Repo.
articles.author: stefan karger

articles.title: Typewriter Web Component: A Web Component for a Customizable Animated Text Effect
articles.description: The project only lives on CodePen for now but it’s a neat effect. The demo allows you to interactively start, pause/resume, complete, and reset the animation. There’s also a scrubber and buttons to go to specific progress points.
articles.author: Luis Alberto Martinez Riancho codepen

articles.title: Nxtlify: A Web-Based Theme Builder for Nuxt UI
articles.description: Work with existing Nuxt UI components, customizing the different CSS variables and view the changes live on the page. Currently supports about 18 components.
articles.author: Toni Kritz
